customManagers.description: Workload image refs — kept identical in the manifest AND versions.yaml (bin/version-lint enforces the mirror), so match both globs in one manager => one PR rewrites both files.

Platform Helm chart pins in versions.yaml (mirror of the argocd/apps targetRevisions). Inline `# renovate:` comments carry datasource/depName/registryUrl; depName matches the chart name the argocd manager uses, so both files bump in one PR.

packageRules.description: Default soak for everything (one component = one PR; Renovate's default is per-dependency).
ArgoCD = CURRENT policy. CRDs stay argoproj.io/v1alpha1 even on 3.x, so a chart bump won't silently break refs — take bumps promptly, no soak.

etcd / k3s = CONSERVATIVE policy (the multi-node-on-one-host etcd-quorum race makes these highest-blast-radius). Visible-but-not-rushed: long soak, kept off the demo path. NOTE: k3s itself is a host-bootstrap pin (Ansible, versions.yaml k3s.version) with no Renovate datasource, so this rule guards any etcd/k3s *container* that ever enters scope.
ArgoCD must NOT manage the k3s-bundled Traefik CRD/controller (architecture Networking rule) — never open a Traefik bump PR against this repo.

Pin-by-digest stays pinned: refresh the digest when the upstream tag moves. Images pinned `name@sha256:` with NO tag are immutable and not Renovate-trackable (no tag to follow) — pin as `name:<tag>@sha256:<digest>` to make a workload trackable. Honest ceiling, documented in the runbook.
